verses.text: Take Aaron and his sons to minister unto Me — Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, Ithamar. Make holy garments for Aaron for glory and beauty. Tell the wise hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, to make ephod, breastplate, robe, broidered coat, mitre, girdle — gold, blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen. Guidance: God calls a family to the priesthood and dresses them for glory and beauty. Holiness is not only inward — it has a look.

Take Aaron and his sons to minister unto Me — Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, Ithamar. Make holy garments for Aaron for glory and beauty. Tell the wise hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, to make ephod, breastplate, robe, broidered coat, mitre, girdle — gold, blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen. Guidance: God calls a family to the priesthood and dresses them for glory and beauty. Holiness is not only inward — it has a look.

Make the ephod of gold, blue, purple, scarlet, fine twined linen — cunning work, two shoulderpieces joined, curious girdle of the same. Take two onyx stones, engrave Israel's names — six on each, in birth order. Set in gold ouches on the ephod's shoulders, for memorial. Wreathen gold chains fasten them. Guidance: Aaron carries Israel's names on his shoulders. Twelve tribes pressed against his strength every time he ministers. God remembers His people through His mediator.

Make the breastplate of judgment — cunning work, gold, blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, foursquare doubled, a span. Four rows engraved with the twelve tribes: sardius/topaz/carbuncle; emerald/sapphire/diamond; ligure/agate/amethyst; beryl/onyx/jasper. Gold chains and blue laces bind it to the ephod. Put Urim and Thummim within. Aaron bears Israel's judgment on his heart continually. Guidance: Twelve names on shoulders; twelve on heart. Strength and love together. Urim and Thummim — Lights and Perfections — give God's decisions when the path is unclear.

Make the robe of the ephod all of blue. A hole for the head, bound that it tear not. On the hem, pomegranates of blue, purple, scarlet, with golden bells between — alternating round. Aaron wears it to minister; his sound is heard going in and out before the LORD — that he die not. Guidance: A blue robe, bells and pomegranates — the priest moves and the people hear him. The sound is grace; while it rings, he lives.

Make a plate of pure gold; engrave like a signet: HOLINESS TO THE LORD. Put it on a blue lace upon the mitre, on the forefront. It shall be on Aaron's forehead, that he may bear the iniquity of the holy things — accepted before the LORD. Guidance: HOLINESS TO THE LORD shines from the priest's forehead. The very offerings of God's people carry iniquity; the priest bears it for them, that their worship is received.

Coat of fine linen, mitre of fine linen, girdle of needlework. For Aaron's sons, coats, girdles, bonnets for glory and beauty. Anoint, consecrate, sanctify them. Make linen breeches covering loins to thighs. They wear them in the tabernacle or near the altar — that they bear not iniquity and die. A statute forever. Guidance: Even the priests' undergarments are commanded. God's house is no place for exposure or shame; modesty is part of the holiness of approach.
